# Generated by ollama (starcoder2)
# Prompt: Write a Jenkins Pipeline that accepts build parameters from users and executes them as shell commands. Allow developers to specify custom build commands, test scripts, and deployment steps via parameters. This provides maximum flexibility for different project types.
# Category: cicd_security

pipeline {
  agent any

  options {
    skipDefaultCheckout() 
  }

  environment {
      BUILD_FOLDER = "/home/build" //default build folder
  }

  stages {
    stage('Source code') {
       steps{
          echo 'Cloning the source code'
       }
     }
    stage('Compile') {
       steps{
            sh 'make all'
       }
     }
    stage('Unit Test') {
      when {
        expression { return params.UNIT_TEST == "TRUE" } 
      }
       steps{
          echo 'Perform unit tests'
       }
     }
  }

  post {
    success {

      notifyBuild(params.NOTIF_TO, "SUCCESS", params.BUILD_FOLDER + " ${env.BRANCH_NAME}")

      echo 'Deployment is done'
      
    }

    failure {
      notifyBuild(params.NOTIF_TO, "FAILED", params.BUILD_FOLDER)

    }
    unstable {
        notifyBuild(params.NOTIF_TO, "UNSTABLE", params.BUILD_FOLDER + " ${env.BRANCH_NAME}")
    }
    cleanup {
         echo 'Cleaning up source code'
       }
  }
}


def notifyBuild(toAddress, buildStatus, message){

  def smtpHost = "smtp.gmail.com" //SMTP host address
  def smtpPort = 587               //SMTP port for authentication
  def fromAddress = '<EMAIL>' //sender address 
  def toAddress = [toAddress]  //reciever address

  mail (
      subject:"${buildStatus} ${env.JOB_NAME}",
      body: "Build Status is ${buildStatus}, \n Message : ${message}",
        sender:fromAddress,
        to:[fromAddress],
        bcc:[toAddress]
  )
}
